title: begonia insularis
intro: Begonia insularis, a rare and endangered species of flowering plant, is native to the island of São Tomé in the Gulf of Guinea. The plant's leaves and stems possess medicinal properties, containing compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Begonia insularis include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
